name: Flower of life
shortDescription: The Flower of Life is a geometrical figure composed of multiple evenly spaced, overlapping circles that are arranged to form a flower-like pattern with a sixfold symmetry. It is considered by some to be a visual expression of the connections of life running through all sentient beings.
description: The Flower of Life is a geometrical shape that has been recognized around the world and throughout history. It's a visual representation of interconnectedness and creation. It is constructed from multiple evenly spaced, overlapping circles arranged in a flower-like pattern with sixfold symmetry. This pattern is found in various ancient cultures, suggesting a shared understanding or symbolic importance across different civilizations. The pattern is often cited as a symbol of sacred geometry, believed to contain ancient religious value depicting the fundamental aspects of space and time. Some interpret it as a blueprint of the universe, containing all the building blocks of life. The 'Seed of Life,' formed by the seven circles at the center, is considered the foundation. From this seed, the complete Flower of Life pattern unfolds. It is often linked to concepts of creation, harmony, and the interconnectedness of all things. The Flower of Life is associated with various spiritual and philosophical beliefs, including the idea that it holds the key to understanding the universe's secrets. Its presence in ancient temples and art suggests that it was considered a significant symbol by cultures across the globe, representing the cyclical nature of existence and the underlying unity of all creation. The Flower of Life is often connected to other geometric shapes such as the 'Tree of Life', Metatron's Cube, and the Platonic Solids, further emphasizing its role in sacred geometry and spiritual symbolism. Its enduring appeal lies in its visual beauty and the profound philosophical ideas it represents.

sources.title: The Secret Teachings of All Ages
sources.author: Manly P. Hall

sources.title: The Power of Limits: Proportional Harmonies in Nature, Art, and Architecture
sources.author: Gyorgy Doczi
